title: erythea edulis
intro: Erythea edulis, also known as creosote bush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of erythea edulis include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. Erythea edulis is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be used topically in salves and creams.
